sentence: The report was written by the manager
hint: Passive: subject + was/were + past participle + by agent
explanation: Past passive: 'was written'. The agent comes after 'by'.

sentence: English is spoken in many countries around the world
explanation: Present passive: 'is spoken' (not active 'speaks'). No agent needed here.

sentence: The new hospital was built last year
explanation: Past passive: 'was built'. 'Build' is irregular: built (not 'builded').

sentence: The students are taught by experienced teachers
explanation: 'Students' is plural: 'are taught' (not 'is taught').

sentence: The window was broken during the storm
explanation: Past passive without agent: 'was broken during the storm'.

sentence: The cake was made by my grandmother
explanation: Past passive: 'was made'. 'Make' is irregular: made (not 'maked').

sentence: These cars are manufactured in Germany
explanation: 'Cars' is plural: 'are manufactured' (not 'is manufactured').

sentence: The meeting was canceled due to bad weather
explanation: Past passive: 'was canceled'. 'Due to' introduces the reason.

sentence: The letter was sent to the wrong address
explanation: 'Send' is irregular: sent (not 'sended').

sentence: Coffee is grown in tropical countries
explanation: Present passive for general facts: 'is grown'.

sentence: The homework must be completed before Friday
hint: Modal passive: modal + be + past participle
explanation: Modal passive: 'must be completed'. Modal + be + past participle.

sentence: The children were taken to the hospital by ambulance
explanation: 'Children' is plural: 'were taken' (not 'was taken').

sentence: This song was recorded in a famous studio
explanation: Past passive: 'was recorded'. Place at the end.

sentence: The rules should be followed by all employees
explanation: Modal passive: 'should be followed'. Agent after 'by'.

sentence: The bridge was designed by a famous architect
explanation: Past passive with agent: 'was designed by'.

sentence: Tickets can be purchased online or at the door
explanation: Modal passive: 'can be purchased'. Two options joined by 'or'.

sentence: The exam results were announced this morning
explanation: 'Results' is plural: 'were announced' (not 'was announced').

sentence: The food is prepared fresh every day
explanation: Present passive for routines: 'is prepared'.

sentence: The project will be finished by the end of the month
hint: Future passive: will + be + past participle
explanation: Future passive: 'will be finished'. 'By' for deadline.

sentence: The museum was visited by thousands of tourists last summer
explanation: Past passive with agent and time expression at the end.
